title: alyssum procumbens
intro: Alyssum procumbens, also known as Creeping Alyssum, is a low-growing, spreading annual plant that produces small white flowers and is native to Europe and North Africa. The medicinal properties of Alyssum procumbens include its potential antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to support respiratory health, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote a good night's sleep. Alyssum procumbens can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
